name: ordinary-men-reserve-police-battalion-101-and-the-final-solution-in-poland
description: Christopher R. Browning's Ordinary Men: Reserve Police Battalion 101 and the Final Solution in Poland — a Holocaust history and social psychology toolkit investigating how 500 middle-aged German policemen became mass murderers, killing 38,000 Jews and deporting 45,000 more, revealing the terrifying power of conformity, obedience, and the banality of evil.
useCase: Understand how ordinary people become perpetrators of genocide — the psychology of conformity, obedience, and desensitization that turned average middle-aged men into mass murderers.
knownRisks: This book describes graphic violence and mass murder — content may be distressing
Browning's thesis challenges both the 'evil Nazi' and 'forced to obey' narratives, which may feel uncomfortable
The book has been criticized by some scholars who emphasize ideological motivation more than Browning does
